articles.title: ZHA 3D-prints voluptuous chair from recycled plastic
articles.summary: Architecture studio ZHA, formerly Zaha Hadid Architects, has worked with Spanish brand Nagami to launch the 3D-printed Echo Chair made from recycled plastic. The sculptural seating design features an arching backrest that extends to the ground in one plane and a seat that scoops up at the sides to hug the sitter. Although these two elements The post ZHA 3D-prints voluptuous chair from recycled plastic appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Contemporary Room Dividing Screens by Fold Studio
articles.summary: Dezeen Showroom: Australian design brand Fold Studio has released a series of freestanding folding screens comprising hardwood frames stretched with fabric or woven cord. Fold Studio founder and in-house designer Pauline Ollmann created the brand's Woven-Fold and Fabric-Fold collections, which share American White Oak, American Black Walnut or American Ash frames and raw brass hinges. articles.summary: In this week's comments update, readers are discussing Nokia's nostalgic dumbphone that features a dedicated AI assistant button. Launched by tech company HMD, these dumbphones were designed to stick with the simplicity and affordability of retro brick phones, while incorporating certain comforts of modern life. articles.title: HTA Design restores Crystal Palace's historic concrete dinosaurs
articles.summary: UK studio HTA Design has renovated a group of 19th-century dinosaur sculptures as part of a wider revamp of Crystal Palace Park in south London. HTA Design led the renovation of the 172-year-old artworks, believed to be the world's first life-sized dinosaur models, which had fallen into a state of disrepair in recent years. The The post HTA Design restores Crystal Palace's historic concrete dinosaurs appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Bokey Grant creates "low-tech" sanctuary in Australian Flame Zone
articles.summary: Architecture studio Bokey Grant has designed a monolithic house on the edge of a bushland escarpment in New South Wales, Australia. Named EA House (Esther and Albert's), the 170-square-metre family home is located on the land of the Gundungurra people in the Southern Highlands. "The core concept was to design a home that was experimental The post Bokey Grant creates "low-tech" sanctuary in Australian Flame Zone appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: "Stadiums are the most significant expression of monumentality in our time"
articles.summary: As part of Dezeen's Future Stadium series, academic Benjamin Flowers considers the role of stadiums as one of the last monumental architectural forms that demands affection from the public. In any given city around the world, the single largest and most expensive building a visitor encounters won't be a church, or a major museum, or The post "Stadiums are the most significant expression of monumentality in our time" appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.summary: Dezeen School Shows: a contemporary furniture collection that puts a twist on the classic look of traditional Parisian pieces is among the projects showcased at this year's New Designers. Also featured are playful seating for children and an accessible toaster with large buttons, designed to be suitable for all ages. articles.title: Studio Font perches cliffside home in Mexico on inhabitable concrete columns
articles.summary: Local practice Studio Font has completed Casa Diez Pies, a home in Mexico that steps down a lush coastal cliff supported in 10 concrete columns. Overlooking the Pacific Ocean in Mazunte, the 250-square-metre home was raised on 10 large concrete columns to lessen its impact on the steeply sloping site's vegetation. These large columns, which The post Studio Font perches cliffside home in Mexico on inhabitable concrete columns appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Studio Fuksas' Rike Park Cultural Complex Approved for Demolition by Tbilisi City Hall
articles.summary: On July 13, 2026, Tbilisi City Hall issued a permit to dismantle Rike Park's tube-shaped Music Theater & Exhibition Hall . The complex, designed by the Italian firm Studio Fuksas , was never officially opened since its completion in 2012, around the same time as the firm's Tbilisi Public Service Hall . The design has been a source of controversy between authorities and citizens since its commission in 2011, when it was built during the government of the United National Movement (UNM), and was suspended after the change of government in 2012. The two structures, often referred to as the "Rike T

articles.title: Museums in Debate: What Ecuador's MuNA Reveals About National Identity
articles.summary: The announcement of the winning proposal for Ecuador's new National Museum (MuNA) sparked one of the country's most significant architectural debates in recent years. The public response has since reshaped the competition itself while also raising broader questions about how a national museum gives architectural form to an interpretation of a nation. While the discussion began with a single design, it exposed a challenge that extends far beyond Ecuador .

articles.title: House Hotelito / FIVE OH FIVE
articles.summary: A building designed in the 1930s by the architect Domingo Pisaca y Burgada blends its aesthetic with modernism and eclecticism. The structure is part of a group of ten villas, or "hotelitos," as they were commonly called. These "hotelitos" have earned much of the neighborhood its designation as a Site of Cultural Interest under the category of the Historic Ensemble known as the Los Hoteles–Pino de Oro district.

articles.title: Be-Fore Facility / a.co.lab
articles.summary: The 'Be, Fore' is a renovation project for idle areas of the Sihwa Industrial Complex sewage treatment plant. Although Siheung-si had planned a water-themed park for nearby families, we found upon visiting the site that this former industrial facility possessed unique qualities within the urban context. The site still bears traces of its past, and we felt that planning a program by a few decision-makers in a short time would serve only as an image, lacking practicality and ethics.

articles.title: Stream Restaurant / Atelier Guo
articles.summary: Along the Li River, at the foot of the mountains, the restaurant occupies a long, narrow site pressed against a rocky cliff. A flood-control canal cuts across it—part natural, part engineered—tracing the contours of the mountain. The restaurant's spatial organization, podium, and roof all extend from the geometry of this canal, abstracting and echoing the mountain's profile.

articles.title: Refurbishment Popocatépetl 35 / Studio Font
articles.summary: Hipódromo Condesa emerged in the 1920s as one of the first experiments in modern urbanism in Mexico City. The neighborhood occupies the former horse racetrack of the capital and, from that preexistence, constructs a singular urban character: a curved, organic, and continuous layout structured by what is now Avenida Ámsterdam, preserving the memory of the equestrian circuit.

articles.title: MA House – Atibaia / Estúdio Rossi Arquitetos
articles.summary: Sited on a sloping plot in a gated community in Atibaia, Brazil, the MA Residence is organized around the geography of the lot, the landscape, and the domestic program, forming a permanent home designed for year-round use.

articles.title: Residência MA – Atibaia / Estúdio Rossi Arquitetos
articles.summary: Implantada em um terreno em aclive em um condomínio residencial de Atibaia , a Residência MA se organiza a partir da leitura da geografia do lote, da paisagem e do programa doméstico, dando forma a uma morada definitiva, concebida para uso contínuo ao longo do ano.

articles.title: Treinta y Nueve Árboles / Grizzo Studio
articles.summary: This house is located in the forest of Cariló , in a field full of vegetation, where the sunlight, filtered by the crowns of dozens of trees, stands out on a ground covered with climbers. Our project started from preserving this, the house adapted to the environment, without needing to impose itself, but rather it was intertwined with the trees, and thus it becomes an atrium for contemplation of nature, this house directs us into the forest so that we can let's experiment.

articles.title: Blue House / extrastudio
articles.summary: This project is the first in a series of three residences we named casas poveras. Shaped in a time of uncertainty, yet refusing to compromise on scale, these houses were stripped down to the essentials, acquiring an unexpected, raw, and in-tense character.

articles.title: Westlake 66 Mixed Use Complex / KPF
articles.summary: Westlake 66 is a new model for urban connectivity, landscape-driven design and computationally informed architecture, reinvigorating a deteriorated neighborhood and establishing a new civic heart in one of China's most dynamic cities.

articles.title: Laila Gohar Brings Her Playful World to Rocco’s Beverage Fridge
articles.summary: The food artist reimagines Rocco’s signature beverage fridge in a glossy Black Mirror finish and accompanying custom champagne coupe designed for entertaining

articles.title: Lindye Galloway Reimagines Two Upper West Side Penthouses as One Home
articles.summary: Thoughtful millwork, natural stone, and custom furnishings unify two pre-war Manhattan apartments into a warm, contemporary home for a young family.

articles.title: Alex Proba and Salehe Bembury Turn Their Signature Motifs Into Home Decor
articles.summary: Soft Imprints translates the duo’s shared instincts for color, pattern, and tactility into a limited-edition collection of rugs and pillows.

articles.title: Willem de Haan's Highrise Campsite
articles.summary: This Highrise Campsite is by Dutch artist Willem de Haan . Built from construction scaffolding, camping gear and string lights, it's 11m (about 36') tall and hosts 12 tents, in a minimal footprint. De Haan erected it next to a river in the Dutch countryside. It's not a practical structure, but is more of a commentary: "Even in this seemingly peaceful landscape next to the IJssel river," he writes, "the battle for scarce space is taking place." It does remind me of The Stacks from Ready Player One.

articles.title: Snow Peak's Tiny LED Lantern
articles.summary: This Micro Hozuki is by Japanese outdoor gear brand Snow Peak . It's a diminutive rechargeable LED light that puts out 27 lumens, good enough for campsite task lighting or to read by. Unfortunately it's not rechargeable, but runs on two CR1632 coin-cell lithium batteries. It provides two hours of runtime on High, and 31 hours on Low (2.8 lumens). What you may not be able to tell by the photos is how incredibly tiny it is: The light runs $15.

articles.title: Striking Chinese Industrial Design: Aulumu's M10 Powerbank
articles.summary: It was a matter of time before China began producing original, world-class industrial design, which will hopefully allow them to put the piracy rep in the rearview mirror. This M10 powerbank, by Shenzhen-based tech accessories company Aulumu , is a striking example of homegrown design. Rather than creating a boring brick, the company pursued the cyberpunk aesthetic, yielding a techno-futuristic asymmetrical body and an innovative feature: The lanyard is actually the charging cable. The device can also charge wirelessly, featuring a MagSafe-compatible charging pad. Lastly it's TSA-compliant for

articles.title: China's New Coastal Installation Looks Like It Belongs in Space - Yanko Design
articles.summary: China has unveiled a striking coastal installation that resembles a futuristic spacecraft, blending artistic ambition with architectural innovation. The structure features layered glass mosaics and kinetic panels that dance with sea breezes, creating an immersive visual experience for visitors. Demonstrating the intersection of technology and design, it signals a shift toward experimental public art in marine environments.

articles.title: Meet the Red Dot Award-winning expression of Meghalaya’s place, purpose and precision
articles.summary: An innovative design project from Meghalaya won a Red Dot Award, showcasing the state’s cultural identity through precision engineering and sustainable materials. The piece integrates local motifs with modular construction, enabling flexible adaptation across various community spaces. This accolade highlights the growing influence of regional design practices on global platforms.

articles.title: To the Red Dot Newsroom
articles.summary: An invitation or announcement to the Red Dot Design Award's newsroom, offering insights into award processes and recent winners. The piece likely serves as a call for designers to submit projects or engage with media coverage. With limited details, it functions as a routine internal communication.

articles.title: General press information on the Red Dot Design Award
articles.summary: The Red Dot Design Award released general press material outlining award criteria, submission guidelines, and judging procedures. This information serves journalists and designers seeking clarity on the prestigious program’s integrity and influence. It largely serves as a background briefing for upcoming award cycles.
